title: citrus × insitorum
intro: Citrus × insitorum, also known as Meyer lemon, is a hybrid citrus plant resulting from the cross between a lemon (Citrus limon) and an orange (Citrus sinensis). The medicinal properties of Citrus × insitorum include its high content of flavonoids, limonoids, and ascorbic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming Citrus × insitorum include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and support digestive health due to its high vitamin C and flavonoid content. Citrus × insitorum can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and also used in culinary and traditional medicine applications.
